title: Add fold-safe empirical hazard and remaining-stint priors
rationale: `PitNextLap` is naturally a discrete-time hazard. Survival literature and recent F1 pit-stop work both frame next-event prediction as hazard/horizon estimation rather than plain classification. Historical pit-window tables should be more informative than raw TyreLife alone.
implementation_hint: Inside each fold, compute smoothed OOF hazard tables for buckets such as `(Year, Race, Compound, Stint, TyreLife_bin, RaceProgress_bin)` with hierarchical backoff to global/compound-only priors. Derive features like next-lap hazard prior, cumulative survival to current lap, expected laps remaining, and percentile within historical stint length.
expected_effect: Directly encodes pit windows and should improve separation near the decision boundary, especially late-stint laps.
risk: Easy to leak if priors are computed on full data; sparse race-driver cells need aggressive smoothing/backoff.
